annotation.text: General Resolutions are non-codified legislative actions that have the force and effect of law and are used to express the opinion or will of the majority of the Chickasaw Tribal Legislature. They may include approval of leases, contracts, applications for program or service funding, gubernatorial appointments, and other business that does not require placement in the Chickasaw Nation Code. General Resolutions are provided for historical and legal reference; not every General Resolution is codified. Unless a resolution states otherwise, a General Resolution takes effect upon gubernatorial concurrence, valid veto override, or operation of law under the applicable return-required rule.
